rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM L ROBERTS et al VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 10, 2015 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Bakersfield Pipe and Supply, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent William Roberts was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories and decedent's deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, they identified persons with knowledge of decedent's exposure to defendant's asbestos-containing products and defendant did not demonstrate that the identified persons do not have such knowledge. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Defendant's evidence is insufficient to demonstrate that (1) plaintiffs have identified the wrong entity, and/or (2) the gaskets purchased at the store at issue did not contain asbestos. However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, decedent's deposition testimony and the declarations of Victor Figueroa and Keith Roussell would create a triable issue whether decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing gaskets attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' objections to Dan Byrum's declaration are overruled as to objection no. 1, sustained as to objection no. 2 only as to his statement "and review of BPS corporate records," and sustained as to objection nos. 3 and 4. If the Court got to plaintiffs' evidence, defendant's objections to the declarations of Victor Figueroa and Keith Roussell would be overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
